steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content:
The passage explicitly describes the 47% finding as undermining the basking shark's past classification as a full ectotherm.

This is significant because the passage established that regional endotherms have >30% compact tissue to maintain blood pressure — so a full ectotherm with 47% directly contradicts that original category.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: The statement "It is, by itself, sufficient to invalidate how the basking shark has been categorized in the past" is correct: the 47% is sufficient to invalidate the past classification. The others fail by reversing the logic, appealing to percentages not in the passage, or misidentifying the questioned classification. That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
